aruba.name: Aruba
aruba.tagline: The 'One Happy Island' – A Year-Round STR Powerhouse.
aruba.long_overview: Aruba stands alone in the Caribbean for its geographical advantage: it sits below the hurricane belt. This ensures that your property remains operational and insurable year-round. The market is driven by high repeat-visitor loyalty, primarily from the US Northeast, creating a predictable and stable rental environment.
aruba.regulations: Foreigners enjoy the same property rights as citizens. There are no restrictions on foreign ownership of land. Short-term rentals are well-regulated but encouraged, provided you pay the 9.5% tourist tax and environmental levy.
aruba.seasonal_trends: Unlike other islands, Aruba has a 'flat' seasonality. While winter is peak, summer occupancy remains high due to South American travelers and families avoiding hurricane risks elsewhere.
aruba.investment_case: Aruba is the 'low-volatility' play. It is perfect for investors prioritizing cash-flow consistency and low weather-related risk over speculative capital appreciation.

dominican-republic.name: Dominican Republic
dominican-republic.long_overview: The DR is the most visited destination in the Caribbean. With a massive pipeline of new infrastructure in Punta Cana and Miches, the entry price for luxury condos remains the lowest in the region, offering a high 'yield-on-cost' for early investors.
dominican-republic.regulations: The CONFOTUR law is the primary driver here, offering 15 years of 0% property tax and 0% income tax for buyers in certified tourism projects.
dominican-republic.seasonal_trends: Strong winter peak from Dec–March. Growing 'Digital Nomad' trend in Samaná is starting to fill the shoulder seasons (May–June).
dominican-republic.investment_case: The DR is a high-yield, growth play. It is best for investors looking for aggressive tax incentives and the lowest price-per-square-foot in the tropics.

the-bahamas.name: The Bahamas
the-bahamas.tagline: Ultra-Luxury Proximity to the US Mainland.
the-bahamas.long_overview: With 700+ islands, the Bahamas offers everything from high-density Nassau condos to ultra-exclusive Exuma villas. Proximity to Florida (a 30-minute flight) makes it the premier choice for the weekend luxury rental market.
the-bahamas.regulations: The International Persons Landholding Act governs purchases. While straightforward, buyers should account for a VAT of 10% on property transfers.
the-bahamas.seasonal_trends: Extreme peaks during US holidays and Spring Break. Many 'Out Island' rentals command $2,000+ per night during the festive season.
the-bahamas.investment_case: The Bahamas is an 'Appreciation & Lifestyle' play. It offers high barrier-to-entry luxury that holds value exceptionally well during global downturns.

turks-and-caicos.name: Turks & Caicos
turks-and-caicos.tagline: The Platinum Standard of Caribbean ADR.
turks-and-caicos.long_overview: TCI is a high-ADR (Average Daily Rate) market. Grace Bay is consistently voted the world's best beach, allowing owners to command premium pricing that outweighs the lower occupancy rates compared to Aruba.
turks-and-caicos.regulations: No annual property taxes and no capital gains taxes. TCI is a true tax-neutral jurisdiction for real estate holding.
turks-and-caicos.seasonal_trends: Highly seasonal. Revenue is heavily concentrated in the 'Dry Season.' Owners often use the hurricane-prone months for property maintenance.
turks-and-caicos.investment_case: TCI is for the high-net-worth investor. It provides a sophisticated legal framework and the highest daily rental rates in the Western Hemisphere.

nc-coastal.name: NC Coastal
nc-coastal.long_overview: From the Outer Banks to Wrightsville Beach, the NC Coast is a premier 'drive-to' market. Large multi-generational beach houses (8+ bedrooms) are the dominant earners, catering to massive family reunions.
nc-coastal.regulations: Regulatory risk is higher here due to local town ordinances (SROs). Investors must carefully vet beach nourishment taxes and local occupancy tax requirements.
nc-coastal.seasonal_trends: Extremely compressed season. 70% of annual revenue is typically earned between Memorial Day and Labor Day. Large 'Event Homes' are now extending this into the wedding season (Oct/Nov).
nc-coastal.investment_case: NC Coastal is a 'Cash Machine' play. While the season is short, the weekly rental rates for large homes are massive, and domestic travel is incredibly resilient.
